page: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpdsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores using the 64-bit ARM Neoverse-N2 architecture clocked at 3.4 GHz. With one thread per core, the system avoids simultaneous multithreading overhead. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache per core, and a shared 128 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 3780 GB of bundled local storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-threaded performance and memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier ratings in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 7276.23 GB/sec and large block uncached read bandwidth at 467.26 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. Single-core performance is moderate, with Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core benchmarks falling into the average category. Software compilation via Clang and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression also register in the top tier.

The ARM64 architecture provides high resource density and power efficiency, translating to qualitative cost advantages for highly parallelized workloads. However, hardware-level tradeoffs exist due to the lack of x86-specific instruction sets; there is no support for AVX, AVX-512, AMX, or BMI2 instructions, though hardware-accelerated AES cryptography is present. This makes the server less suitable for workloads relying on x86 vector extensions, while it excels in memory-intensive and multi-threaded integer operations.

Given the balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, large local storage capacity, and top-tier database operations benchmark, this server is configured for database hosting, multi-threaded software compilation, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v6 is an ARM64-based virtual machine from the Dpdsv6 family, powered by a 64-core Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz on the Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and includes 3780 GB of bundled local storage. The architecture lacks x86 vector extensions like AVX and AMX but supports AES cryptography. In benchmarks, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and memory read/write bandwidth, alongside strong database operations and static web serving throughput. Conversely, single-core metrics remain in the average tier. This resource profile makes the instance highly efficient for parallelized workloads, including database management, multi-threaded compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v6 is a 64-core ARM64 virtual machine with 256 GB RAM and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated cores, balanced memory, and high-capacity local storage for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 Neoverse-N2 cores running at 3.4 GHz with no multithreading overhead.
256 GB of system memory paired with 3780 GB of bundled local storage.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing.
Lacks x86 vector extensions such as AVX and AMX, but supports hardware AES cryptography.
Suited for database hosting, multi-threaded software compilation, and web serving.
